title: Simple Open-Top Wood Toy Box on Casters
summary: An open-top rolling toy box — cedar-picket sides on a 2x2 leg frame, plywood bottom, and four locking casters so the whole bin rolls to wherever the mess needs to go.
description:
This toy box skips a lid entirely — it's an open bin on wheels, built so cleanup means rolling it to the toys rather than carrying a box to them. The plywood bottom and four 2x2 legs go together first, one screw per leg from underneath, and every following step references flush and square off that base.

The end panels go on before the sides, and the plan is specific about checking square as you attach the topmost fence picket especially — that top board is the one most likely to visibly telegraph a slightly out-of-square leg. The remaining side pickets center with an even gap on either side rather than each having a fixed position, so minor width variation between boards doesn't accumulate into a lopsided gap by the last board.

**If using cedar fence pickets, sand them well and wear a respirator or mask while doing it** — cedar dust is a genuine respiratory irritant in a way pine dust generally isn't, and a box built for a kid's room is worth the extra minute of protection during the one dusty step.

materials.name: Cedar fence pickets (not dog-eared) or 1x6, 8 ft
Non-marking caster wheels, with screws
materials.note: Make sure the screw heads are large enough for the caster holes, or use washers.

cutList.material: Cedar fence pickets (not dog-eared) or 1x6, 8 ft
Cedar fence pickets (not dog-eared) or 1x6, 8 ft
cutList.note: Measure and cut to fit the actual assembled base.

steps.title: Attach the legs to the base
steps.body: Attach the four legs to the plywood base with 2" wood screws and wood glue driven from the underside — one screw per leg is enough.

steps.title: Attach the end panels
steps.body: Attach the end cedar fence pickets to the legs with 1-1/2" screws, two per end of each picket, four screws total per picket. Keep the legs square with the pickets as you attach them, especially the top picket.
materials: Cedar fence pickets (not dog-eared) or 1x6, 8 ft

steps.title: Attach the side panels
steps.body: Repeat the same process for the side fence pickets.
materials: Cedar fence pickets (not dog-eared) or 1x6, 8 ft

steps.title: Center the remaining panels
steps.body: Center the remaining fence pickets, keeping an even gap on both sides, and attach with 1-1/2" screws, two per end of each picket.
materials: Cedar fence pickets (not dog-eared) or 1x6, 8 ft

steps.title: Add casters and finish
steps.body: Attach the caster wheels to the base with the caster wheel screws. If using cedar, sand thoroughly and wear a respirator or mask while sanding — cedar dust is a real respiratory irritant.

images.alt: Open-top rolling toy box with cedar picket sides on casters
